title: Writing - Part 1: Space Exploration
description: Your class has been discussing whether space exploration is worth the investment compared to solving problems on Earth.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the points in your notes. You should explain which point you think is most important, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Money spent on space could be better used to solve poverty and climate change on Earth.
Space exploration leads to technological innovations that benefit everyday life.
Working together on space missions encourages peace between nations.

structure.intro: Introduce the debate around space exploration and state which two points you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first point (e.g., scientific discoveries) with supporting examples.
structure.body2: Discuss the second point (e.g., cost) and explain which you consider more significant.
structure.conclusion: Summarise your argument and state your overall opinion.

content.modelAnswer:
Space exploration has always sparked debate. Supporters argue it drives progress, while critics question the enormous expense involved. In this essay, I will consider the scientific benefits of space research and the financial cost it requires.

Undoubtedly, space exploration has led to remarkable scientific advances. Technologies such as GPS, satellite communications and even memory foam were all developed as a result of space programmes. Furthermore, studying planets and stars helps scientists understand the origins of life itself. These discoveries have a genuine impact on our daily lives and future generations.

However, the cost of space exploration cannot be ignored. Billions of pounds are spent on missions that could alternatively fund hospitals, schools or climate change initiatives. Many people argue that, with so many pressing problems on Earth, it seems irresponsible to prioritise space.

In my opinion, although scientific progress is valuable, we should address urgent earthly challenges first. Space exploration should continue, but with more international collaboration to share costs and maximise benefits for all of humanity.
